# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Laravel service class that sends SMS notifications via Twilio API. The service should handle authentication and message sending. Configure the class to read Twilio credentials from the appropriate configuration source.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Twilio\Rest\Client;
use Exception;

class TwilioSmsService
{
    protected $twilioClient;
    protected $fromPhoneNumber;

    public function __construct()
    {
        $accountSid = config('services.twilio.sid');
        $authToken = config('services.twilio.token');
        $this->fromPhoneNumber = config('services.twilio.from');

        if (empty($accountSid) || empty($authToken) || empty($this->fromPhoneNumber)) {
            throw new Exception('Twilio credentials are not set in the configuration.');
        }

        $this->twilioClient = new Client($accountSid, $authToken);
    }

    /**
     * Send an SMS message using Twilio.
     *
     * @param string $to The recipient's phone number.
     * @param string $message The message body.
     * @return bool True if the message was sent successfully, false otherwise.
     * @throws \Exception If the message sending fails.
     */
    public function sendSms(string $to, string $message): bool
    {
        try {
            $this->twilioClient->messages->create(
                $to,
                [
                    'from' => $this->fromPhoneNumber,
                    'body' => $message
                ]
            );
            return true;
        } catch (Exception $e) {
            // Log the exception for debugging
            // For example: Log::error("Failed to send SMS to {$to}: {$e->getMessage()}");
            throw new Exception("Failed to send SMS to {$to}: " . $e->getMessage(), $e->getCode(), $e);
        }
    }
}
